era: 1959-1966 core. Surf/Garage. E=6 -- Seattle chapter of Northwest Sound. Seafair / Dolton Records.

theory_nodes.concept: Northwest Sound geographic trio complete: Tacoma (Sonics 241, Wailers 242) + Seattle (Frantics 243) + Portland (Kingsmen 244). The Frantics represent Seattle's chapter of the regional movement: more polished, surf-influenced, less extreme than Tacoma output.
theory_nodes.description: Frantics as Seattle chapter of Northwest Sound.

theory_nodes.concept: Dolton Records (Liberty subsidiary) connection: same label network as The Ventures. 'Walk Don't Run' (Ventures, Dolton) and Frantics recordings in same distribution ecosystem. National reach through Liberty.

Surf-garage synthesis at E=6: cleaner guitar tones than Sonics (E=8), more aggressive than Ventures. The Dick Dale tremolo-and-reverb technique applied to Northwest Sound energy. The middle ground of the 1959-1966 era.
theory_nodes.description: Surf-garage synthesis as distinct aesthetic position.

cross_references.relationship: Tacoma's extreme counterpart. Same era; E=8 vs E=6 reflects Tacoma extremism vs. Seattle polish.
Shared Dolton Records connection. The Ventures built global career from same distribution network.
Geographic arc completion: Tacoma + Seattle + Portland = full Northwest Sound map.

sps_pair.pairing_note: E=6 vs E=1 (TIES MINIMUM). FIRST PETROMYZONTIDAE. The surf-garage Seattle band paired with the most ancient jawless fish in the engine. Five-point spread.
